title: CERN & the Mandela Effect
summary: 'All timelines are converging into one single reality' — the collider as a timeline-merger, and false memories as its exhaust.
markdown:
Asked in the [Los Angeles Alien Event session](/sessions/2024-04-12-the-los-angeles-alien-event/)
why people report **Mandela effects** — shared memories of a past that doesn't match the
record — *"when CERN is turned on,"* Unimetrix 1 gives the archive's tidiest explanation of
the phenomenon: the collider is a **timeline-merger**. *"All timelines are converging into one
single reality,"* and the misremembered book titles and logos are the seams — memories
carried over from [parallel branches](/lore/parallel-timelines/) that no longer exist as
separate lines.

It is a small claim with the archive's whole physics inside it. The record already holds that
timelines are continuously [edited and course-corrected](/lore/the-chronicle/) from the
future; the CERN answer adds a *present-tense, human-built* machine to the same machinery —
convergence as an industrial process, with the public's déjà vu as its measurable exhaust.
Your memory isn't wrong, the record says; your *timeline* changed underneath it.

One session, one question, one answer — but it gives the archive's readers a name for
something half of them will have experienced, and folds a famous internet mystery into the
[timeline war](/lore/project-reclamation/) without breaking stride. Preserved as spoken, not
independently verified — which, for a claim about unreliable memory, is a caveat with a
certain symmetry to it.
